design_goal: Build a three-stage AEP creation flow (structured form + AI generation, live chat test, natural-language refinement) that abstracts a complex AI persona data model so a non-technical security manager can produce a structurally complete, behaviorally validated, compliance-enforced persona in under 2 hours without seeing JSON.
user_segments: Customer — Security Manager (primary: creates, tests, refines, publishes)
Dune Operator (internal: full access including raw JSON, validation override, operator refinement)
Dune Admin (platform-level: operator permissions + template management, global ban list)
constraints: No raw JSON visible to customers — only Dune Operators see raw JSON in a dedicated technical panel
Stage 2 must use the full production persona runtime — identical to live campaign behavior
Generation: P95 45s without examples, 90s with OCR — labeled progress stages required, static spinner not acceptable
Stage 3 diff view is consequential — per-field accept/reject must be clearly legible
Published AEPs are immutable — editing requires cloning to new version
Validation is a hard gate — blocking errors prevent publish; warnings require explicit per-item acknowledgment
Compliance fields (instigation threshold, frameworks, termination triggers) are consequential and must be treated accordingly
Templates must be clearly framed as starting points requiring customization before generation
Phase 1 target: Q3 2026 — do not design for Phase 2 features
selected_strategy: Structured 6-section form + example upload → AI generation + natural-language refinement loop
selected_strategy_rationale: The PRD v0.2 confirms this approach based on analysis of 6 real customer AEP requirements. The structured form ensures completeness of capture. Example upload is the highest-quality signal for persona calibration. AI generation eliminates manual JSON authoring. Live test with full production runtime is a genuine market differentiator. Natural-language refinement with per-field diff review keeps customers in control without requiring JSON knowledge.

alternative_strategies.name: Pure plain-language brief → AI generates
alternative_strategies.summary: Admin writes a free-text scenario description; AI generates the full AEP from the brief
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: Insufficient for the structural complexity of AEP requirements. Real accounts require capture of crown jewels, compliance frameworks, cultural nuances, termination triggers, and more. A free-text brief produces incomplete personas requiring many correction loops — the problem the product is trying to solve.

alternative_strategies.name: Fully structured form, no AI
alternative_strategies.summary: Admin fills every field manually; system assembles AEP from form data without LLM generation
alternative_strategies.rejected_because: The AEP schema has 20+ field groups including a full 6-state state machine, detection arrays, and cultural nuance packages. Manual authoring is what the current paper process does and takes ~1 day per AEP. This eliminates the product value proposition.

tradeoffs: Six-section form is thorough but long — experienced users may find it slow for repeat AEPs; Clone + Template paths mitigate this
Generation quality is load-bearing — if the generated persona is poor, Stage 2 and Stage 3 are fighting an uphill battle; generation prompt quality is critical
Stage 3 diff complexity — a single refinement may cascade across many fields; flat diff list may be overwhelming; consider section grouping
Template adoption depends on template quality — generic templates that don't reflect target customer segments will be skipped
assumptions: Stage 1 form is the authoritative source for all AEP content — no separately authored raw prompts by customers
Stage 2 is a manual customer-plays-employee test, not AI-vs-AI
6 fixed conversation states (1_INITIAL through 6_VULNERABILITY) — customers configure scripts, not states
Published AEPs are locked to version at campaign creation — no hot-swap mid-campaign
Library is per-org scope (one library per tenant) pending PRD clarification
No hard cap on Active AEPs per tenant — archiving is the management mechanism
open_issues: [Critical — PM] Is a Reviewer ever mandatory (blocking gate) or always optional? Affects publish flow and Pending Review state
[Critical — Both] Multi-channel deployment model — simultaneous channels in one campaign, or one-channel-per-campaign?
[Medium — Eng] Stage 2 LLM model — full production vs. lighter model; affects unlimited session positioning
[Medium — PM] Instigation threshold enum — three values sufficient given real account variation?
[Medium — Eng] Auto-save behavior for Stage 1 form — background auto-save or explicit Save Draft CTA?
risks: Generation quality is the product's core promise — form structure, example upload, and generation prompt quality are all load-bearing
Stage 3 diff legibility — if hard to parse, customers bypass review; needs dedicated design work
Refinement loop without exit signal — customers may publish too early without a quality/completion indicator
Template quality mismatch — templates not reflecting target customer segments will undermine reuse metric
ds_patterns_used: wizard (three-stage: Form+Examples, Live Chat Test, Prompt Refinement)
table (AEP Library list view with sort, filter, search)
modal (publish confirmation, archive confirmation, validation warning acknowledgment)
badge (Status: Draft / Pending Review / Active / Archived; Channel; Compliance Framework)
drawer (AEP detail — may be full-page detail rather than drawer given content volume)
empty state (AEP Library first visit, no Active AEPs in campaign creation)
CodeEditor or TextArea (operator raw JSON panel — Advanced mode only)
new_patterns_required: Generation progress screen with labeled stages (15–90s wait with advancing stage labels) — requires DS review
Split-pane layout for Stage 2 (AEP summary left + live chat right) and Stage 3 (AEP summary left + refinement/diff right) — requires DS review for responsive handling
Stage 3 diff view with per-field accept/reject — new pattern; requires DS review
Archetype starter buttons in Stage 2 (one-click first-response injection) — new micro-interaction
Conversation state label indicator in Stage 2 chat (real-time state display) — new pattern
Repeatable row input for Systems at Risk (add/remove application rows) — may need DS pattern definition
Example upload with per-file OCR status (uploading → OCR processing → ready / failed) — new pattern
next_actions: Resolve critical open issues: mandatory reviewer gate (PM) and multi-channel deployment model (Both) before finalizing the publish flow
Design AEP Library table first — status model, row actions, filter/search, empty state
Design Stage 1 form — progressive disclosure strategy for 6 sections, help text approach, template pre-fill path
Design generation progress screen — labeled stages, partial failure handling
Design Stage 2 (Live Chat Test) — state label panel, archetype starters, session controls, split-pane
Design Stage 3 (diff view) — per-field accept/reject, section grouping, guardrail block messaging
Audit Stillsuit DS v2 for: table pattern, wizard step bar, split-pane, diff view, repeatable row input
